title: FERC Order 887 — Internal network security monitoring for bulk electric systems
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=FERC CIP, clause=Order887, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Order887.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that FERC Order 887 (Internal network security monitoring for bulk electric systems) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.44.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.44' running on catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention).
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: BES network flow data, ESP firewall logs, IDS/IPS within Electronic Security Perimeters, network device syslog from control centres.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=ot_network sourcetype IN (bes_network_flow, esp_firewall, ot_ids) earliest=-24h
| eval anomalous=if(action IN ("blocked", "denied", "alert") OR protocol NOT IN ("modbus", "dnp3", "iec61850", "iccp", "opc-ua", "https", "ssh"), 1, 0)
| eval internal_traffic=if(src_zone="esp" AND dest_zone="esp", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_flows sum(anomalous) AS anomalous_flows sum(internal_traffic) AS internal_flows dc(src_ip) AS distinct_sources dc(dest_ip) AS distinct_destinations BY esp_zone, protocol
| eval anomaly_pct=round(100*anomalous_flows/total_flows, 1)
| where anomalous_flows>0
| sort -anomalous_flows
| table esp_zone, protocol, total_flows, anomalous_flows, anomaly_pct, internal_flows, distinct_sources, distinct_destinations
description: Monitors internal network traffic within BES Electronic Security Perimeters for anomalous or unauthorised protocols and flows per FERC Order 887 requirements for internal network security monitoring.
value: FERC Order 887 requires utilities to implement internal network security monitoring within ESPs beyond the perimeter. Continuous network flow analysis within control system networks provides evidence of monitoring capability.
implementation: (1) Deploy network flow collection within ESP zones; (2) normalise protocol, action, src_zone, dest_zone; (3) schedule every 15 minutes; (4) escalate anomalous internal flows immediately. Indexes required: `index=ot_network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=bes_network_flow`, `sourcetype=esp_firewall`, `sourcetype=ot_ids`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `anomalous`, `anomalous_flows`, `anomaly_pct`, `distinct_destinations`, `distinct_sources`, `esp_zone`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
grandmaExplanation: We watch traffic inside the bulk-electric security zones so we can show internal network monitoring rules are met.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure data sources: BES network flow data, ESP firewall logs, IDS/IPS within Electronic Security Perimeters, network device syslog from control centres.
• See docs/implementation-guide.md for app layout and inputs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deploy network flow collection within ESP zones; (2) normalise protocol, action, src_zone, dest_zone; (3) schedule every 15 minutes; (4) escalate anomalous internal flows immediately. Indexes required: `index=ot_network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=bes_network_flow`, `sourcetype=esp_firewall`, `sourcetype=ot_ids`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (save as report or alert; tune time range and thresholds):

```spl
index=ot_network sourcetype IN (bes_network_flow, esp_firewall, ot_ids) earliest=-24h
| eval anomalous=if(action IN ("blocked", "denied", "alert") OR protocol NOT IN ("modbus", "dnp3", "iec61850", "iccp", "opc-ua", "https", "ssh"), 1, 0)
| eval internal_traffic=if(src_zone="esp" AND dest_zone="esp", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_flows sum(anomalous) AS anomalous_flows sum(internal_traffic) AS internal_flows dc(src_ip) AS distinct_sources dc(dest_ip) AS distinct_destinations BY esp_zone, protocol
| eval anomaly_pct=round(100*anomalous_flows/total_flows, 1)
| where anomalous_flows>0
| sort -anomalous_flows
| table esp_zone, protocol, total_flows, anomalous_flows, anomaly_pct, internal_flows, distinct_sources, distinct_destinations
```

Understanding this SPL

**FERC Order 887 — Internal network security monitoring for bulk electric systems** — FERC Order 887 requires utilities to implement internal network security monitoring within ESPs beyond the perimeter. Continuous network flow analysis within control system networks provides evidence of monitoring capability.

Output fields and breakdowns should match the panel fields listed in **Visualization**.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a compliance or VSOC dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Consider: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `anomalous`, `anomalous_flows`, `anomaly_pct`, `distinct_destinations`, `distinct_sources`, `esp_zone`.
